hero.title: Sappa.
hero.lede: Acoustic panels grown from hemp — a fast-renewing natural fibre, bound with a cement-free mineral binder. For walls and ceilings, brought to Denmark by Beyond Rational.

hero_note: Surfaces shown are an indicative rendering of the finish range. Final samples and the full colour range on request.

points.title: A fast-renewing raw material
points.body: Hemp needs little water, no pesticides, and reaches full height in months, not years — capturing atmospheric carbon roughly twice as efficiently as forestry across its growing cycle.

points.title: Zero-waste by construction
points.body: The panels are pressed from stem chips — the residue left over once fibre is stripped from the plant for other industrial uses. Material that would otherwise be discarded becomes the product.

points.title: Cradle to cradle
points.body: A cement-free mineral binder holds the hemp chips together, with no synthetic flame retardants added — the panel is naturally fire-resistant and recyclable at end of life.

rows.value: Naturally fire-resistant — no flame retardants added

gallery_head.body: Images are managed in the CMS — add or replace them at /admin.

cta.lede: Want to hear when Sappa is ready? Send us a line and we'll keep you posted.
